user_query:
I pre-captured our OpenClaw environment outputs so you can diagnose without running any commands. Please read all files under input/ and produce two artifacts:

1) Write a full OpenClaw Recovery Report to output/recovery_report.txt that follows the exact structured format:
- Header/footer lines
- STATUS (PASS/FAIL/DEGRADED)
- OS, STATE_DIR, CONFIG
- GATEWAY (<reachable|unreachable> with port and pid if known)
- CHANNELS summary
- AGENTS count
- SECURITY (CRITICAL count)
- Then one ISSUE block per problem with: COMPONENT, SEVERITY, FINDING, EVIDENCE (max 5 lines pulled from the input files), RECOVERY, and ACTION_REQUIRED (exact command(s) for a human to run)

Use only the supplied inputs; do not execute anything. Base your diagnosis on:
- input/env.txt (OS, state dir, config path, gateway port)
- input/openclaw_status.txt (overall status)
- input/ports.txt (listener checks for :18789 and :18790)
- input/services.txt (Windows Scheduled Tasks inventory)
- input/security_audit.txt (security findings)
- input/logs.txt (recent errors)
- input/config.json (check for BOM and syntax)

Your report must, at minimum, identify and include ISSUE blocks for all of the following if supported by the evidence:
- Gateway unreachable on port 18789 (ECONNREFUSED)
- A channel down condition for Telegram (while Discord is OK)
- Config BOM problem causing JSON parse error (Unexpected token at position 0)
- Memory fts unavailable (fts5 missing)
- Security audit CRITICAL items (count = 2)

Include actionable, report-only commands appropriate to the detected OS (Windows in env.txt) in ACTION_REQUIRED:
- For Gateway/service: use schtasks commands (e.g., inventory and run the active task)
- For channel diagnostics: include a safe probe like "openclaw status --deep"
- For config fix: include the Node one-liner BOM removal script with the actual config path from env.txt
- For security fixes: include icacls-based permission tightening commands (report-only)

2) Write a machine-readable issues file to output/issues.json containing an array of issue objects. Each issue must include at least: component, severity, finding, and a short evidence field. Ensure components include: "Gateway", "Channel", "Config", "Memory", and "Security".

Do not assume any paths beyond what’s in input/env.txt. Do not run commands; treat everything as offline analysis. Make sure EVIDENCE lines in the report quote exact lines from the relevant input files.
